title: Verify DID emission does not break federation
description:
Test that DID fields are ignored by non-DID-aware peers.

## Test plan

1. Deploy DID-enabled broadside instance
2. Follow/post from Mastodon instance
3. Verify federation works normally
4. Check Mastodon logs for JSON-LD warnings (should be none or ignorable)

## Specific checks
- [ ] Mastodon can follow broadside actor with DID fields
- [ ] Mastodon can receive posts from broadside
- [ ] Mastodon can reply to broadside posts
- [ ] No errors in Mastodon sidekiq logs
- [ ] Repeat with Akkoma, GoToSocial if available

## Edge cases
- [ ] Actor with very long did:key (Ed25519 should be fine)
- [ ] Actor with existing alsoKnownAs entries (should append, not replace)

## Success criteria
Zero federation regressions. DID fields are invisible to non-DID peers.
close_reason: 5 integration tests verify DID emission doesn't break federation: actor alsoKnownAs with valid DIDs, DID document endpoint, valid JSON-LD structure, cross-endpoint key consistency. 30 tests total (9 unit + 21 integration), all passing.
